title: Scaling to Multiple Inputs
concept: Multiple linear regression predicts an output by combining information from several features rather than relying on just one input.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the primary benefit of using adaptive optimizers like AdaGrad and RMSprop during training?

options.text: They automatically adjust learning rates for each parameter individually based on past updates.
They draw a perfect linear regression line automatically on the first attempt.
They discard all features that have small values to speed up training.

exercises.explain: Correct! Adaptive optimizers scale steps for each parameter individually so that features updated less frequently get larger adjustments.

cards.title: Beyond a single feature
cards.body: If you want to estimate a home's value, is its size in square feet the only thing that matters? Of course not! Neighborhood safety, school ratings, and number of bedrooms all play a part.

cards.title: Multiple Linear Regression
cards.body: Multiple linear regression is a machine learning technique that uses two or more inputs to predict a single numerical output. It extends simple regression by assigning a unique weight to each input.

cards.title: Baking a perfect cake
cards.body: Think of multiple regression like a recipe. One ingredient alone doesn't decide the cake's quality. Instead, flour, sugar, and baking time each have their own weight in determining the final taste.

cards.title: Predicting gas mileage
cards.body: To predict a car's highway fuel efficiency, a model might combine the car's weight, its engine displacement, and its aerodynamic drag coefficient to calculate a single estimated MPG value.

cards.title: More is not always better
cards.body: You might think adding as many features as possible always improves a model. However, adding irrelevant features can confuse the model and lead to overfitting on noise.

cards.title: Simple vs. Multiple Regression
cards.body: Comparing the scope of the models:

left.text:
Only 1 input feature
Predicts 1 output
Example: Price from size

right.text:
2+ input features
Predicts 1 output
Example: Price from size + bedrooms

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is the best example of a problem suited for multiple linear regression?

options.text: Predicting a crop's yield using rainfall, soil nitrogen levels, and average temperature.
Predicting whether a transaction is fraudulent or legitimate based on location.
Classifying images of animals into cats or dogs.

exercises.explain: Correct! Multiple regression predicts a continuous numerical output (crop yield) using multiple input features.

exercises.prompt: True or false: Multiple linear regression can only predict categorical outputs like 'Yes' or 'No'.
exercises.explain: False. Just like simple linear regression, multiple linear regression is used for predicting continuous numerical values.

exercises.sentence: While simple regression uses one input, multiple regression combines ___ input features to make a prediction.
exercises.explain: Multiple linear regression uses two or more input features to predict a target value.

exercises.prompt: Which of these regression models represents multiple linear regression?

options.text: A model predicting movie ratings using only the budget size.
A model predicting movie ratings using budget size, cast popularity, and genre classification.
A model predicting if a user will click an ad or not using their browser history.

exercises.explain: Correct! A model using multiple features (budget, cast popularity, and genre) to predict a continuous score (movie rating) is multiple linear regression.

exercises.prompt: You are hired by a streaming service to predict how many hours a user will stream next month. You have data on their age, monthly subscription tier, and minutes watched last month. How should you approach this?

options.text: Build a multiple linear regression model that weights all three features together to predict the hours streamed.
Build three separate simple linear regression models and pick the one with the highest age coefficient.
Ignore the age and subscription tier, because simple regression is always more accurate than multiple regression.

exercises.explain: Precisely! Multiple linear regression allows you to leverage all available relevant features simultaneously, combining their individual influences to make a more accurate prediction. Outstanding start to Multiple Linear Regression!
